items.title: Pakistan PM Shehbaz Sharif declares 'final, agreed upon text' of US-Iran peace deal reached — 'Peace has never been this close'; Pakistan acting as mediator
US senior official says 80-85% confident of signing deal 'in coming days' — 'Not 100%'; confidence rose from 75% morning to 80-85% afternoon; Khamenei reportedly 'comfortable'
Iran FM Araghchi says MoU with US 'has never been closer' — announces remote signing if deal agreed; urges media not to speculate on content
IRGC-affiliated Fars News: 'high probability' Iran will approve MoU text — Tasnim: 'still requires review and finalization by relevant institutions'; first positive signal from IRGC-aligned media
Netanyahu declares 'Israel not a party to MoU' — praises Trump's commitment to nuclear restrictions, enrichment dismantling, missile limits, proxy end; not informed in advance of strike cancellation
MoU contents detailed: Hormuz reopen immediately without tolls, pre-war shipping in 30 days; 60-day ceasefire extension; nuclear commitments; sanctions relief tied to compliance
Oil crashes further: Brent toward $88/bbl (~4% drop Friday), lowest in nearly 2 months on deal optimism — analysts: Brent could fall to ~$70 if Hormuz reopens (Fitch)
US moving forward with signing ceremony plans — Switzerland possible venue; still needs final Iran approval; Vance to attend
Pakistan PM 'final, agreed upon text' of US-Iran deal — Korean coverage (Finanshyeonnyusu)
MoU contents analysis: frozen assets, crude sanctions relief — Korean analysis (Finanshyeonnyusu)
Vance says deal 'still TBD' — cautious contrast with Pakistan PM's definitive 'final text' statement; message gap between mediator and parties persists
Israel-Lebanon: at least 12 killed in continued Israeli strikes on southern Lebanon, including Sidon — LAF warns may freeze cooperation with ceasefire committee
Trump 'Iran war deal... weekend European signing possible' — Korean follow-up (Newspim)
